name: fastv_gqa_control
note: FastV ACCURACY-ONLY control on GQA (keep-tokens=576 => FastV OFF). The no-prune LLaVA-1.5-7B baseline via the FastV repo's llava lib (same env path as pruned runs -> identical tokenizer/preprocessing). Sanity check: this should match the vLLM r0 GQA acc (~0.585) within +/-2% (the two paths differ only in attn impl: fastv env uses eager+patched, vLLM uses SDPA; small numeric diffs expected). If it diverges >5%, flag a scorer/preprocess mismatch before trusting pruned numbers.

name: fastv_gqa_r50
note: FastV @ r50 (keep 288/576) on GQA. THE ACCURACY ANCHOR: this is the canonical TF compressor at 50% prune. Compare to (a) proxy probe r50 (0.565, -2.0%) and (b) v1 true_cls r50 (to be run). FastV prunes INTRA-LLM at layer 2 (attention-rank on the last decode token) -- a DIFFERENT mechanism from boundary CLS-attn -- so it sets the bar our boundary method should approach/match at iso-budget. Published FastV GQA at ~50% prune is ~60% on full val (we're on a 200-subset, expect noise).

name: fastv_gqa_r75
note: FastV @ r75 (keep 144/576) on GQA. The high-prune anchor. Compare to proxy r75 (0.470, -11.5%) -- FastV's intra-LLM attention-rank should degrade more gracefully here than boundary CLS-attn at extreme prune (this is FastV's known strength). If our v1 true_cls r75 approaches FastV r75, that's a strong result (boundary method matching intra-LLM method at extreme budget).

name: fastv_textvqa_r50
note: FastV @ r50 on TextVQA. OCR-robustness anchor: proxy r50 was 0.530 (-2.5%). FastV is known to degrade on OCR at high prune -- this characterizes whether intra-LLM pruning preserves text-region tokens better than boundary CLS-attn. The OCR comparison (FastV vs v1 true_cls) is a key table row.
